On this page

FAQ ​

Frequently asked questions about AI Image Renamer.

Is this plugin free? ​

Yes! The plugin is 100% free and open source (GPL-2.0). It uses the Groq API, which offers a generous free tier for personal and commercial use.

Do I need a Groq API key? ​

Yes. You need a free API key from console.groq.com. Registration is free and takes less than a minute. No credit card required.

What happens if the API fails? ​

The plugin gracefully falls back to the original filename. Your upload will never fail due to API issues — the image is always saved, just with its original name.

Which AI models are available? ​

You can choose between two Llama 4 vision models:

  • Maverick — High-performance model with 128 experts, best for detailed analysis
  • Scout — Lightweight model with 16 experts, optimized for speed

Both models are free to use through Groq's API.

Does this work with existing images? ​

Currently, the plugin only processes new uploads. Bulk renaming of existing images is planned for a future Pro version.

Can I customize the AI prompt? ​

Yes! Use the air_prompt filter to modify or completely replace the prompt. See the examples page for code snippets.

Can I disable renaming for specific uploads? ​

Yes. You can either:

  1. Toggle "Enable Auto-Rename" off in the settings before uploading
  2. Use the air_should_process_upload filter to programmatically skip certain uploads

Is my API key secure? ​

Yes. API keys are encrypted using the defuse/php-encryption library before being stored in the database. For maximum security, you can define your encryption key in wp-config.php:

php
define( 'AIR_ENCRYPTION_KEY', 'your-key-here' );

See the Settings page for more details.

Does this affect site performance? ​

The API call happens only during upload and typically takes 1–3 seconds. Regular site visitors will not notice any performance impact. There is zero frontend overhead.

What file types are supported? ​

JPEG, PNG, WebP, AVIF, and GIF. You can enable or disable specific types in the settings.

Can I use this on multiple sites? ​

Yes! Each site needs its own configuration, but you can use the same Groq API key across multiple WordPress installations.

What data is sent to Groq? ​

When you upload an image, the plugin sends:

  • The image data (base64 encoded)
  • A text prompt asking the AI to describe the image

This data is only sent during upload, and only for enabled file types. No data is stored on external servers by this plugin. See the Groq Privacy Policy for how Groq handles data on their end.

How do I report a bug? ​

You can report bugs through:

  • WordPress.org Support Forum
  • GitHub Issues
